fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.helpText: From AWS IAM -> Users -> Security credentials -> Access keys

fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.helpText: From the same IAM access key creation page

fields.label: Region
fields.helpText: Default AWS region for API calls

fields.label: Account ID
fields.helpText: 12-digit AWS account ID. Found in the top-right menu of the AWS Console.

healthcheck_config.description: Validates AWS credentials via STS GetCallerIdentity

metadata.summary: Amazon Web Services access for compute, billing, storage, and other AWS services.
metadata.setup_guide:
1. Log in to the AWS Console.
2. Go to IAM -> Users -> select your user (or create one).
3. Under Security credentials, click 'Create access key'.
4. Choose 'Application running outside AWS'.
5. Copy the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key.
6. Your Account ID is in the top-right menu of the AWS Console.
7. Grant the IAM user permissions needed for your use case (e.g., ce:GetCostAndUsage for billing).
